content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN, and a positive integer KKK.

It is guaranteed that 1≤Ai≤K1 \leq A_i \leq K1≤Ai​≤K for every index iii from 111 to NNN.

You can do the following **at most once**:


Choose an index iii (1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N) and a value xxx (1≤x≤K1 \leq x \leq K1≤x≤K).
Then, set Ai:=xA_i := xAi​:=x.
Find the **maximum** possible value of the sum of adjacent differences of AAA after performing this operation at most once.

That is, maximize the quantity

∑i=1N−1∣Ai−Ai+1∣\sum_{i=1}^{N-1} |A_i - A_{i+1}|i=1∑N−1​∣Ai​−Ai+1​∣
Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the length of the array and the maximum allowed integer KKK, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, the elements of array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the maximum possible sum of adjacent differences of AAA after replacing at most one element.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 100010001000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: It's best to leave the array unchanged, giving us a difference of ∣1−5∣=4|1-5| = 4∣1−5∣=4.
Test case 222: It's optimal to set A2:=1A_2 := 1A2​:=1, giving us the array [7,1,7][7, 1, 7][7,1,7]. The sum of adjacent differences is 6+6=126 + 6 = 126+6=12.
Test case 333: It's optimal to set A3:=20A_3 := 20A3​:=20, to obtain [18,3,20,4,19][18, 3, 20, 4, 19][18,3,20,4,19]. The sum of adjacent differences is 636363.
